event: Angel of Certitude on the Horizon
symbols: luminous sphere with eye — divine omniscience, the all-seeing gaze of God or a questioning celestial presence
dark sky — the mystery and unknowability of the divine
stone face emerging from darkness — ancient, enduring spiritual presence
water at bottom — the primordial deep, the waters of creation
bright cloud above — the shekinah glory, divine presence in luminous obscurity
description: A dark, predominantly black composition with three main elements. At top, a bright, cloud-like luminous mass glows against the deep black sky. In the center, a perfectly round white sphere contains what appears to be a single eye gazing to the side. At lower right, a large stone or sculptural face emerges from the darkness, partially illuminated, with an ancient, weathered expression. Dark water stretches across the bottom of the composition. The overall effect is mysterious, dreamlike, and cosmic.
theological_description: Redon's visionary composition evokes the biblical tradition of divine mystery revealed through darkness and light. The luminous eye-sphere recalls Zechariah's vision of 'the eyes of the Lord, which range throughout the whole earth' (Zechariah 4:10), as well as the all-seeing eye of God that 'searches every heart and understands every plan and thought' (1 Chronicles 28:9). The ancient stone face emerging from darkness suggests the 'Ancient of Days' (Daniel 7:9) — the eternal divine presence that precedes and underlies all creation. The dark waters below recall Genesis 1:2: 'darkness was over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of God was hovering over the waters.' The title speaks of 'certitude' and 'questioning' — the tension between faith and doubt, between divine assurance and human incomprehension. This symbolist vision captures the experience described in Psalm 139: 'Where can I go from your Spirit? If I make my bed in the depths, you are there' — even in the most impenetrable darkness, the divine eye watches.

scripture_refs.reason: Darkness over the face of the deep — the primordial darkness and waters evoked in the composition
Even darkness is not dark to you — the divine presence penetrating the deepest darkness
The Ancient of Days — the weathered face suggesting an eternal, primordial divine presence
The eyes of the Lord are in every place — the all-seeing eye-sphere

_meta.title: On the Horizon, the Angel of Certitude, and in the Dark Sky, A Questioning Glance
